verses.text: Law of the trespass offering — most holy. Killed where the burnt is killed; blood sprinkled round the altar. Offer all the fat — rump, kidneys with fat, caul above liver. Priest burns on the altar. All male priests eat — most holy. As sin offering, so trespass: same law. The priest that atones has it. Guidance: Trespass and sin offerings follow the same shape — God treats wrongs against neighbor and against Himself with the same seriousness. Restitution plus blood; both layers are required.

The priest that offers any man's burnt offering keeps the skin. Every meat offering baked in the oven, fried, made in the pan is the priest's who offers it. Every meat offering mingled with oil or dry — all sons of Aaron share equally. Guidance: The priest gets the skin, the loaf, the leftovers. God arranged for those who serve at the altar to be supported by the altar. Ministry has wages, and they come from the very thing you offer.

Peace offering of thanksgiving: unleavened cakes with oil, wafers, fine flour fried. Also leavened bread. One from each — heave to the priest. Thanksgiving flesh eaten same day. If vow or voluntary — that day or next; third day, burnt. Eaten the third day — abomination. Touching unclean — shall not eat. With uncleanness — cut off. Guidance: A thank-offering is to be eaten today. Joy spoils faster than vow. Don't store gratitude in the freezer — eat it while it's fresh, with the people you love.

The LORD: speak to Israel — eat no fat of ox, sheep, or goat. Fat of a beast that dies may be used otherwise, never eaten. Whoever eats fat — cut off. Eat no blood, fowl or beast, in any dwelling. Whoever eats blood — cut off. Guidance: Two forever-no's: no fat, no blood. The fat is God's portion at the altar; the blood is the life God gave. Eat what is given freely; respect what is sacred.

He that offers peace offering brings it himself. His hands bring the fire offerings — fat with breast. The breast is waved before the LORD; given to Aaron and his sons. The right shoulder is a heave offering. Breast and shoulder — given to Aaron and sons forever. Guidance: The worshipper brings it with his own hands; the priest receives breast and shoulder. Service at the altar is paid in fellowship meals, not silver. The priest is fed at the same table he serves.

This is the law of the burnt offering, meat offering, sin offering, trespass offering, consecrations, peace offerings — which the LORD commanded Moses on Sinai, when He commanded Israel to offer in the wilderness. Guidance: Six offerings end the section. From burnt to peace, every facet of relationship with God has its own gate of approach. Moses learned each on the mountain; Israel will live each in the camp.
